title: Step 4: Click  or  for the changes to take effect.

sections.heading: Required Access
sections.body:
To edit a private endpoint, you must have [Project Owner](<Project Owner>) access to the project.
Users with [Organization Owner](<Organization Owner>) access must add themselves as a `Project Owner`
to the project before setting up a private endpoint.

sections.heading: Procedure
sections.body:
Atlas CLI

To create a new Data Federation private endpoint using the
Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas dataFederation privateEndpoints create <endpointId> [options]

```

To learn more about the command syntax and parameters, see the
Atlas CLI documentation for [atlas dataFederation privateEndpoints create](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-dataFederation-privateEndpoints-create).

Atlas Administration API

Atlas UI

sections.heading: Procedure
sections.body:
To edit a private endpoint through the Atlas Administration API, send a `POST` request
to the `privateNetworkSettings` endpoint with the unique ID of the
private endpoint to edit.

To learn more about the syntax and options, see [API](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-creategroupprivatenetworksettingendpointid).

sections.heading: Procedure
sections.body:
To edit the details associated with a private endpoint from the Atlas UI:

To learn more about the fields you can edit, see [Atlas Data Federation](config-private-endpoint).
